title: E-bicyclist, 14, injured in collision with vehicle in Medway
author: Heather Alterisio
bullets: A 14-year-old e-bicyclist was seriously injured in a collision with a vehicle in Medway on Wednesday.
The accident occurred at the intersection of Village and Oakland streets, requiring MedFlight transport to a trauma center.
Medway Fire Department officials reported the injuries as significant but not life-threatening and thanked public safety partners.

title: Massachusetts tops WalletHub ranking of state economies
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: Massachusetts ranked as having the best state economy in a WalletHub analysis covering 50 states and D.C.
Key factors for Massachusetts' top ranking include high-tech job shares and substantial research investments.
New Hampshire ranked 13th and Maine 48th, showing varied New England state performances in economic evaluation.

title: Turner or Freese? Brad Friedel has a unique perspective on the U.S. World Cup goalkeeper debate
author: Seth Macomber
bullets: Brad Friedel discusses the U.S. goalkeeper competition for the 2026 World Cup between Matt Turner and Matt Freese.
Matt Turner and Matt Freese are contenders due to their performances; Turner has an edge in shot-stopping, Freese in dealing with crosses.
Head coach Mauricio Pochettino uses an analytical approach in selecting starters, aiming for consistent performance in training sessions.

title: Review & setlist: A$AP Rocky turns TD Garden into a massive moshpit
author: Samantha Genzer
bullets: A$AP Rocky's 'Don't Be Dumb' Tour stopped at Boston's TD Garden with a high-energy, theatrical performance.
Rocky performed over 30 songs, including hits from his new album, despite technical issues and a delayed start.
Special effects included fake helicopters and a SWAT-themed entourage, enhancing the concert's dramatic atmosphere.

title: Julian Edelman reacts to Patriots’ A.J. Brown trade: ‘Everybody’s job becomes easier’
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: A.J. Brown joins the Patriots in a trade for a 2028 first-round pick, adding a much-needed No. 1 receiver.
Julian Edelman praised Brown's arrival, saying it will ease pressure on other receivers, likening it to Randy Moss's impact.
Offensive coordinator Josh McDaniels highlighted Brown's strength and competitiveness, comparing him to Rob Gronkowski.

title: BermudAir is adding 3 new destinations from Boston this winter
author: Kristi Palma
bullets: BermudAir will add Belize, Turks and Caicos, and Guatemala City to its Boston route network this winter.
Direct flights from Boston to Turks and Caicos begin Dec. 19, operating on Saturdays through May 1, 2027.
BermudAir's twice-weekly services from Boston to Belize and Guatemala City start on Dec. 19, using Embraer E190 aircraft.

title: Canton police sergeant allegedly linked to Proctor texts has resigned
author: Abby Patkin
bullets: Canton Sgt. Sean Goode resigned amid an internal investigation linked to misconduct with former Trooper Michael Proctor.
Goode had been on paid leave since last fall; the investigation's completion will be reviewed by the POST commission.
Proctor's phone, containing sensitive data, is central to ongoing lawsuits against State Police and Canton for alleged negligence.

title: A$AP Rocky, Rihanna, and Jaylen Brown swung by this Boston establishment
author: John Waller
bullets: A$AP Rocky, Rihanna, and Jaylen Brown visited Hue Boston on a tour stop in the city.
The visit was highlighted by A$AP Rocky and Rihanna autographing the establishment's sign.
A$AP Rocky was in Boston for his "Don't Be Dumb" tour performance at TD Garden.

title: The Red Sox are off to the worst start at Fenway Park since 1932
author: Khari A. Thompson
bullets: The Red Sox have a 9-20 home record, the worst start at Fenway Park since 1932.
Boston ranks second-to-last for both runs (222) and home runs (44) in the league.
The Red Sox have a strong road record at 16-14, fourth-best in the American League.

title: Mass. high school to undergo testing after several female teachers diagnosed with breast cancer
author: Beth Treffeisen
bullets: State health officials will conduct environmental tests at Uxbridge High School following breast cancer diagnoses among female teachers.
Testing will assess indoor and outdoor air quality and check school systems for potential risk factors.
Previous uses of the school's site will be investigated despite the building's 2012 construction date.
